recipe.notes: Tags: Costa Rican, Side, Normal, black beans, peppers
recipe.title: Costa Rican Gallo pinto

ingredient_lines.note: (450 gr.), Fresh are best but most likely you’ll find them dried.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: 1 lb (450 gr.) Black beans. Fresh are best but most likely you’ll find them dried.
8-10 sprigs cilantro (coriander leaf) fresh or frozen, not dried!
½ small red or yellow sweet pepper (optional)
3 cups (700 ml) chicken broth or water
1-3 Tablespoon oil to fry the Gallo Pinto

ingredient_lines.note: If beans are dried, if they are “fresh”, but only from laying out in the sun like they do in Costa Rica, just rise them off., 3 hours.
ingredient_lines.preparation: cover with water and soak overnight, still dried, Drain the beans and add fresh water to an inch (2.5 cm) above the top of the beans and bring to a boil. Cover the pan and reduce heat to very low simmer until beans are soft ~
ingredient_lines.raw_text: If beans are dried, cover with water and soak overnight, if they are “fresh” (still dried, but only from laying out in the sun like they do in Costa Rica), just rise them off. Drain the beans and add fresh water to an inch (2.5-cm) above the top of the beans and bring to a boil. Cover the pan and reduce heat to very low simmer until beans are soft (~3 hours). Add salt.
Chop cilantro, onion, and sweet pepper very fine.

ingredient_lines.note: Add, sweet pepper and cilantro and sauté another 2 minutes., (20-35 minutes). This is also the recipe for Tico rice used in other favorites like tamales.
ingredient_lines.preparation: bring to a boil, cover and reduce heat to simmer until rice is tender
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Add 1 Tablespoon oil to a large pan and sauté the dry rice for 2 minutes over medium high flame then add half of the chopped onion, sweet pepper and cilantro and sauté another 2 minutes. Add water or chicken broth, bring to a boil, cover and reduce heat to simmer until rice is tender (20-35 minutes). This is also the recipe for Tico rice used in other favorites like tamales.

ingredient_lines.note: Once the rice and beans are cooked you can refrigerate or freeze them. Make up small batches of Gallo Pinto when you want it by simply sautéing them together.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Once the rice and beans are cooked you can refrigerate or freeze them. Make up small batches of Gallo Pinto when you want it by simply sautéing them together.

ingredient_lines.input_item: Keep a significant amount of the “black water” with the beans This is what gives the rice its color and some of its flavor. Sauté the rice
ingredient_lines.note: (#1$2-1 cup 120-240 ml)., sweet pepper and cilantro together in vegetable oil for a few minutes.
ingredient_lines.preparation: beans reserved chopped, Sprinkle with a little fresh chopped cilantro just before serving.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Keep a significant amount of the “black water” with the beans (½-1 cup 120-240 ml). This is what gives the rice its color and some of its flavor. Sauté the rice, beans reserved chopped onion, sweet pepper and cilantro together in vegetable oil for a few minutes. Sprinkle with a little fresh chopped cilantro just before serving.
